title: /files route refresh scroll restoration
human_intent: Browser refresh on /files must keep the user at the previous scroll position, matching /settings behavior.
assumptions: ScrollRestoration already persists history-entry + URL fallback for /settings et al.
Earlier fixes (C-036, scroll-anchor opt-out, virtual list spacer hygiene, URL fallback) already neutralize the historical reasons /files was excluded.
/files renders async (React Query + virtualization); a single rAF scrollTo is clamped to top because document height is insufficient on refresh.

risks.risk: Removing the /files exclusion contaminates push-into-/files navigation.
risks.mitigation: history key isolation plus navigationType !== 'PUSH' continues to gate URL fallback; pushed routes still start at top.

risks.risk: Async list mounts later than a single rAF, so restoration still lands at top.
risks.mitigation: Frame-driven retry loop (<= 60 frames ~1s, +/- 2px tolerance) re-issues scrollTo until reached or budget exhausted.

risks.risk: Retry loop fights manual user scrolling.
risks.mitigation: Listen once for wheel/touchmove/keydown and abort restoration immediately on first signal.
Keep original double-rAF fast path for y === 0.

changes: scopeFor(): drop the `/files` early-return so it shares the entry+URL persistence path.
restore(): RAF-retry loop with user-input abort for non-zero targets; preserve double-rAF fast path for y === 0.
Add two regression tests covering /files refresh with same and changed history key.
Extend C-036 to forbid path-based exclusions and mandate the retry+abort policy.

behavior: Refresh on /files restores saved scroll position (entry key retained or recreated).
Push into /files from another route still starts at top.
Manual user scroll during restoration cancels the retry loop.
